title: Transformation of Aureococcus anophagefferens by Electroporation
steps: Filter 100 mL of one week old Aureococcus cultures onto a 45-mm diameter, 0.2-μm nominal pore size GTTP membrane filter for each electroporation you are planning to complete.
Resuspend filtered Aureococcus in 900 μL of 1.0 M sucrose solution by pipetting the sucrose solution against the filter continuously.

Mix 300 μL of the culture resuspension with 10 μg of pUC57 + EGFP Construct. This plasmid contains EGFP under control of the Aureococcus annexin promoter and terminator. Flanking the cassette are DUF285 repeat regions. a
Transfer the Aureococcus / DNA mixture to a pre-chilled electroporation cuvette, and electroporate cells. Electroporation conditions are shown below. Place on ice directly after. a
Transfer Aureococcus into 5 mL of fresh ASP 12 A. ASP12A Recipe for culturing Aureococcus anophagefferens by Ashley A Humphrey
Concentrate 5 mL of the electroporated Aureococcus culture onto a 25-mm diameter, 0.2-μm nominal pore-size GTTP membrane filter disk.
The filter is then placed onto an ASP 12 A + 0.3% Nobel Agar plate. Incubate at 19ºC with a 14:10 (100 μE/m 2 s 2 ) light dark cycle. Growth can be seen after 1 - 2 weeks of growth in the incubator.
creator: Ashley A Humphrey
